updated_conclusion: The earlier scheduler-readiness hand note named the qword row at 0x140C37E20; the exact immediate-store byte target is 0x140C37E21.
summary: Added native Reverser support for x64 RIP-relative immediate memory stores, closing the pe-address-refs gap that hid C6/C7 store references.
Fixed pe-instructions RIP-relative target reporting for ModRM operands with trailing immediates so instruction windows now agree with pe-address-refs.
Pinned the config/player global byte as 0x140C37E21, not the qword base 0x140C37E20.
Mapped four concrete write sites to 0x140C37E21 and five LEA users that pass or materialize the same byte address.
No string literals were found in the touched functions, so semantic names remain inferred from control flow, field offsets, and caller positions rather than symbols.

pe-address-refs: Recognizes C6 /0 RIP-relative imm8 stores as rip-relative-mov-imm-store-byte.
Recognizes C7 /0 RIP-relative imm32 stores as rip-relative-mov-imm-store.
Allows legitimate C6/C7 starts after a previous byte that looks like a REX prefix, while suppressing duplicate inner decodes for other prefixed instructions.
pe-instructions: Adjusts RIP-relative memory targets by the trailing immediate size for group-immediate ModRM instructions.
Emits memory_target_va and memory_target_rva on decoded immediate memory operations.

global_byte.interpretation: adjacent config/player state byte inside a compact global data row
global_byte.confidence: high for exact address, medium for semantic name

write_sites.role: player/config side-effect handler from the scheduler-adjacent config dispatcher
notes: Checks [RCX+0x18] == 1 before setting the global byte.
Uses object+0xF8, clears nested +0x19, walks [object+0x40..+0x48), and sets node+0x78 when node+0x7C == 0x14.
Sets nested +0x18 on success or clears it on the alternate branch.
No direct callsites to the function entry were found.
Function calls 0x1400AE5C0 and then performs two indirect CALL R10 dispatches.
No direct callsites to the function entry were found.
Function shape mirrors 0x1403EC840, including 0x1400AE5C0 and indirect CALL R10 dispatches.
No direct callsites to the function entry were found.
Function has the same helper and indirect dispatch pattern as the set-writer siblings.

call_chain.role: nested helper using the same global byte address

bounded_inference.claim: 0x140C37E21 is a config/player state byte in the settings-side graph, not a JS5/network field.
bounded_inference.basis: All concrete writers are config/player side-effect or callback-style functions; no archive, JS5, packet, or login literals are present.

bounded_inference.claim: 0x1403EC840, 0x1403ECC30, and 0x1403EF3E0 are callback/vtable-reached writers rather than direct scheduler calls.
bounded_inference.basis: No direct callsites to their starts were found, and each ends through repeated indirect R10 calls after helper 0x1400AE5C0.

bounded_inference.claim: 0x1403A5CB0 is the next promising owner/manager path for the global byte.
bounded_inference.basis: It has four LEA materializations of 0x140C37E21 and calls the nested 0x1403A5A10 helper.

next_targets: Trace 0x1403AA880 and its callers to identify the manager that invokes 0x1403A5CB0.
Resolve indirect owners for 0x1403EC840, 0x1403ECC30, and 0x1403EF3E0 through provider/vtable descriptor scans or address references to their entrypoints.
Follow helper 0x1400AE5C0 because all three callback-style byte writers call it before indirect R10 dispatch.
Use the improved pe-address-refs C6/C7 support on nearby qword-row bytes 0x140C37E20..0x140C37E23 to separate sibling config flags.
